Fair Political
Practices Commission
June 1,
1992
Honorable Norman La Force
Counc i imember
City of El Cerrito
802 Baira Drive
El Cerrito,
CA
94530—3002
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No,
A-92—303
Dear Councilmember
La Force:
This is in response to your request for a confirmation of
telephone advice provided to you on May 12,
1992,
concerning your
duties as an El Cerrito City Councilmember pursuant to the
conflict—of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”).
1
This letter confirms that your letter dated May 14,
1992,
accurately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you
on May 12,
1992.2
In our conversation of May 12,
1992,
you asked whether you
had a conflict of interest with respect to a decision of the El
Cerrito City Council affecting a client of your employer.
The
facts are as follows:
You are
a salaried attorney with
CIGNA
Insurance Companies
(CIGNA)
.
Two years ago
CIGNA assigned a case
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Please note that this letter is based on the facts presented to
us.
The Commission does not act as the finder of fact in
-
providing advice.
(In re Ogeisby
(1975)
1
FPPC Ops.
71.)
Moreover, this advice is limited to the provisions of the Act.
428 3 Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660


Our File No.
A-92-303
Page 2
to you in which you defended Security Owners Corporation in a tort
lawsuit.
Currently,
Security Owners Corporation has made an
application to the city for the construction of a subdivision.
You asked whether your prior relationship with Security Owners
Corporation created a conflict of interest in the decision.
As we discussed,
for a conflict of interest to exist with
respect to a specific decision,
you must have an economic interest
which will be foreseeably and materially affected by the decision.
Security Owners Corporation is a source of income to your
employer,
but is not a source of income to you.
(Section
87103(c);
Section 82030.)
Consequently,
Security Owners
Corporation would not be your economic interest.
Your employer is
a source of income and therefore would be an
economic interest of yours.
(Section 87103(c)
and (d).)
Thus,
if
the decision will have a foreseeable and material financial effect
on your employer,
you must disqualify yourself from the decision.
An effect is considered reasonably foreseeable if there is
a
substantial likelihood that it will occur.
Certainty is not
required.
However,
if an effect is only a mere possibility, it is
not reasonably foreseeable.
(In re Thorner
(1975)
1
FPPC Ops.
198.)
Regulation 18702.1 provides that the effect of a decision j
material where
CIGNA is directly involved in the decision.
CIGNA
is directly involved in the decision if CIGNA initiated the
proceeding, is a named party in the proceeding,
or the proceeding
involves the issuance,
renewal,
approval, denial or revocation of
any license, permit,
or other entitlement to,
or contract with,
CIGNA.
This is not the case acording to your facts.
Regulation 18702.2
(copy enclosed)
sets forth the criteria to
determine whether an indirect foreseeable financial effect on a
business entity will be material.
Thus,
for example, if
CIGNA had
an agreement with Security Owners Corporation that when the
subdivision is approved
CIGNA will provide insurance coverage for
the subdivision,
the indirect financial effect on CIGNA may be
great enough to result in disqualification under the appropriate
provision of Regulation 18702.2.
However,
you stated that there
will be no financial effect on your employer from the decision.
Finally,
you would have a nexus in the decision if you were
paid,
by your employer or Security Owners Corporation, to further
the interests of Security Owners Corporation.
This does not
appear to be the case.
You stated that except for the litigation
two years ago,
you have not represented Security Owners
Further,
even if Security Owners Corporation was a source of
income to you, if the income was received more than 12 months
prior to the decision, it would not be a “source of income” as set
forth in Section 87103.


Our File No.
A—92-303
Page 3
Corporation.
You stated at this time you do not know if they are
currently insured by your employer.
Thus,
you would not have a
nexus in decisions affecting Security Owners Corporation.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—59Ol.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
.4

cost of lO per page.

sections.question: You asked whether you had a conflict of interest with respect to a decision of the El Cerrito City Council affecting a client of your employer. The facts are as follows: You are a salaried attorney with CIGNA Insurance Companies (CIGNA). Two years ago CIGNA assigned a case to you in which you defended Security Owners Corporation in a tort lawsuit. Currently, Security Owners Corporation has made an application to the city for the construction of a subdivision. You asked whether your prior relationship with Security Owners Corporation created a conflict of interest in the decision.
sections.conclusion: For a conflict of interest to exist with respect to a specific decision, you must have an economic interest which will be foreseeably and materially affected by the decision. Security Owners Corporation is a source of income to your employer, but is not a source of income to you. Consequently, Security Owners Corporation would not be your economic interest. Your employer is a source of income and therefore would be an economic interest of yours. Thus, if the decision will have a foreseeable and material financial effect on your employer, you must disqualify yourself from the decision. However, you stated that there will be no financial effect on your employer from the decision. Thus, you would not have a nexus in decisions affecting Security Owners Corporation.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a city councilmember who is a salaried attorney at an insurance company have a conflict of interest when voting on a subdivision application from a corporation the councilmember previously represented in litigation two years ago?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No conflict of interest exists because while the employer is an economic interest, the decision will have no foreseeable and material financial effect on the employer, and the councilmember lacks a nexus to Security Owners Corporation since the prior representation ended over 12 months ago and there is no current business relationship.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice. The question and conclusion sections are embedded within the body of the letter rather than appearing under formal section headers. All relevant language has been extracted verbatim from the document. Reference footnotes preserved in conclusion are part of the original text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked whether you had a conflict of interest with respect to a decision of the El Cerrito City Council affecting a client of your employer. The facts are as follows: You are a salaried attorney with CIGNA Insurance Companies (CIGNA). Two years ago CIGNA assigned a case to you in which you defended Security Owners Corporation in a tort lawsuit. Currently, Security Owners Corporation has made an application to the city for the construction of a subdivision. You asked whether your prior relationship with Security Owners Corporation created a conflict of interest in the decision.

CONCLUSION: For a conflict of interest to exist with respect to a specific decision, you must have an economic interest which will be foreseeably and materially affected by the decision. Security Owners Corporation is a source of income to your employer, but is not a source of income to you. Consequently, Security Owners Corporation would not be your economic interest. Your employer is a source of income and therefore would be an economic interest of yours. Thus, if the decision will have a foreseeable and material financial effect on your employer, you must disqualify yourself from the decision. However, you stated that there will be no financial effect on your employer from the decision. Thus, you would not have a nexus in decisions affecting Security Owners Corporation.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice to El Cerrito City Councilmember Norman La Force regarding whether voting on a subdivision application by Security Owners Corporation creates a conflict of interest given his prior representation of that corporation as a salaried CIGNA attorney. The Commission concludes no conflict exists because the employer will suffer no material financial effect and the councilmember has no nexus to the applicant.
